test.name: select_from_1rel_indirect1_alias
test.input: select a from b.c as d

node.text: select a f..m b.c as d

test.name: select_from_into_2rel
test.input: select a into b from c, d

node.text: select a i.. from c, d

test.name: select_from_1rel_group1
test.input: select a from b group by 1

test.name: select_from_1rel_window_empty
test.input: select a from b window c as ()

test.name: select_from_1rel_window_partition1
test.input: select a from b window c as (partition by d)

test.name: select_from_1rel_window_partition1_rows_unbounded
test.input: select a from b window c as (partition by d rows between unbounded preceding and unbounded following)

test.name: select_from_1rel_window_partition1_rows_1preceding_current
test.input: select a from b window c as (partition by d rows between 1 preceding and current row)

test.name: select_from_1rel_2windows
test.input: select a from b window c as (partition by d range between current row and 1 following), e as (partition by f)

test.name: select_distinct_on_from_1rel
test.input: select distinct on (a) a from b

test.name: with_2ctes_select3
test.input: WITH a AS (SELECT 1), b AS (SELECT 2) SELECT 3

test.name: with_recursive_2ctes_select3
test.input: WITH RECURSIVE a AS (SELECT 1), b AS (SELECT 2) SELECT 3

test.name: select_a_orderby_b_c
test.input: select a order by b, c

node.text: select a o..er by b, c

test.name: select_a_orderby_b_c_directions
test.input: select a order by b asc, c desc

test.name: select_a_orderby_b_c_directions_nulls
test.input: select a order by b asc nulls first, c desc nulls last

test.name: select_table_ref
test.input: select 1 into b; select 1 from b;

test.name: select_column_ref_expr_1
select_column_ref_expr_2
test.input: input month type integer using text; select * into countries_joined from country1_weather c1, country2_weather c2 where c1.month = c2.month and c2.month = main.month

test.name: select_generate_series
test.input: select v into a FROM generate_series(0, 10000) as t(v);

test.name: sql_datetime_type_1
test.input: select cast('1' as timestamp with time zone);

test.name: sql_datetime_type_2
test.input: select cast('1' as timestamp (2) with time zone);

test.name: sql_datetime_type_3
test.input: select cast('1' as time with time zone);

test.name: sql_datetime_type_4
test.input: select cast('1' as time (2) with time zone);

test.name: select_distinct_1
test.input: select a is distinct from b;

test.name: select_distinct_2
test.input: select a is not distinct from b;

node.text: a is not d..nct from b

test.name: select_case_1
test.input: select case a when 1 then 2 when 2 then 3 end;

test.name: select_case_2
test.input: select case a when 1 then 2 else 3 end;

test.name: select_func_3
test.input: select somefunc(1) within group (order by c)

test.name: select_knownfunc_28
test.input: select extract(day from a) from foo;

test.name: select_table_func_4
test.input: SELECT * FROM ROWS FROM (generate_series(1) as (a integer), generate_series(2) as (a integer));

test.name: select_table_func_5
select_sample_3
test.input: SELECT * FROM tbl USING SAMPLE 10 PERCENT (bernoulli);

test.name: select_sample_4
test.input: SELECT * FROM tbl USING SAMPLE reservoir(50 ROWS) REPEATABLE (100);

test.name: select_sample_6
test.input: SELECT * FROM tbl TABLESAMPLE RESERVOIR(20%), tbl2 WHERE tbl.i=tbl2.i;

test.name: select_sample_7
test.input: SELECT * FROM tbl, tbl2 WHERE tbl.i=tbl2.i USING SAMPLE RESERVOIR(20%);

test.name: select_join_1
test.input: SELECT * FROM a INNER JOIN b ON c = d

node.text: a INNER JO..b ON c = d

test.name: select_join_2
test.input: SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON c = d

node.text: a JOIN b ON c = d

test.name: select_join_3
test.input: SELECT * FROM a CROSS JOIN b

test.name: select_join_4
test.input: SELECT * FROM a NATURAL JOIN b

test.name: select_join_5
test.input: SELECT * FROM a NATURAL OUTER JOIN b

test.name: select_join_6
test.input: SELECT * FROM a LEFT JOIN b ON foo = bar

error.message: syntax error, unexpected EOF, expecting SCONST or '('

test.name: select_join_7
test.input: SELECT * FROM a RIGHT JOIN b ON c = d

node.text: a RIGHT JO..b ON c = d

test.name: select_join_8
test.input: SELECT * FROM a FULL OUTER JOIN b ON c = d

node.text: a FULL OUT..b ON c = d

test.name: select_join_9
test.input: SELECT * FROM a INNER JOIN b USING (c, d)

test.name: select_row_locking_1
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR UPDATE) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_row_locking_2
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR NO KEY UPDATE) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_row_locking_3
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR SHARE) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_row_locking_4
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR KEY SHARE) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_row_locking_5
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR READ ONLY) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_row_locking_6
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR UPDATE OF foo) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_row_locking_7
test.input: SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM mytable FOR UPDATE OF foo NOWAIT) ss WHERE col1 = 5;

test.name: select_limit_4
test.input: SELECT * FROM foo LIMIT ALL OFFSET 21

test.name: select_limit_5
test.input: SELECT * FROM foo FETCH FIRST 42 ROWS ONLY

test.name: select_limit_6
test.input: SELECT * FROM foo FETCH FIRST 1 ROW ONLY

test.name: select_limit_7
test.input: SELECT * FROM foo FETCH NEXT 1 ROW ONLY

test.name: select_alias_1
test.input: select v into a FROM somefunc() as t(a integer);
